tasks.title: Setup Hardhat Development Environment
tasks.description: Review existing contracts directory structure and understand the UserTransactions.sol contract pattern. Verify Remix IDE deployment approach for Sepolia testnet.
tasks.details:
The project already has a contracts/ directory at the root level (E:\FYP\w3uptime\contracts\) containing UserTransactions.sol, which demonstrates the existing contract patterns.

UserTransactions.sol Analysis:
- Uses Solidity ^0.8.20 (same version needed for W3Governance)
- Imports OpenZeppelin contracts: Ownable, ReentrancyGuard, Pausable, ECDSA, MessageHashUtils
- Implements signature verification pattern using ECDSA.recover() and MessageHashUtils.toEthSignedMessageHash()
- Uses custom errors (InvalidAmount, InsufficientContractBalance, etc.) instead of require statements
- Follows modern Solidity patterns with events for all state changes
- Includes owner-only administrative functions with proper access control

Key Files to Review:
- contracts/UserTransactions.sol:1-173 - Primary reference for contract structure
- Reference in PRD: scripts/prd.txt:192-238 describes expected contracts/ directory structure for W3Governance.sol

Deployment Approach:
- Remix IDE will be used for deployment to Sepolia (no Hardhat setup needed)
- Contract artifacts (ABI, address) will need to be manually extracted from Remix
- Similar to UserTransactions.sol pattern, W3Governance.sol should use OpenZeppelin ^0.8.20 imports

Review Tasks:
1. Document the signature verification pattern used in UserTransactions.sol (lines 102-106, 122-133)
2. Understand the custom error pattern (lines 51-59) for gas efficiency
3. Note the event emission pattern (lines 19-48) for transparency
4. Review constructor pattern (lines 61-73) with validation
5. Understand Ownable pattern from OpenZeppelin (inherited on line 9)
6. Plan W3Governance.sol to follow similar patterns: EIP-712 signatures, custom errors, comprehensive events
tasks.testStrategy:
Review checklist:
1. Confirm contracts/ directory exists at project root
2. Read and understand UserTransactions.sol contract structure
3. Identify OpenZeppelin dependencies already available (@openzeppelin/contracts)
4. Document signature verification pattern for reuse in W3Governance
5. Verify Remix IDE workflow for Sepolia deployment (no compilation test needed)
6. Create notes on contract patterns to replicate: custom errors, event emissions, access control, signature verification
7. Confirm understanding of the difference between MessageHashUtils.toEthSignedMessageHash() (used in UserTransactions) vs EIP-712 typed data (needed for W3Governance)

tasks.title: Create EIP-712 TypedData Definitions
tasks.description: Define EIP-712 typed data structures for gasless vote signatures in packages/common
tasks.details:
Create packages/common/eip712-types.ts file and add export to packages/common/package.json. The project uses ethers.js v6 (^6.15.0 in frontend, ^6.13.4 in validator) and follows the existing patterns in packages/common/contract.ts for contract integrations.

Define VOTE_DOMAIN with:
- name: 'W3Uptime Governance'
- version: '1'
- chainId: 11155111 (Sepolia)
- verifyingContract: placeholder address (to be replaced when governance contract is deployed)

Define VOTE_TYPES following EIP-712 standard:

interface VoteMessage {
  proposalId: string;
  support: boolean; // true = UPVOTE, false = DOWNVOTE
  nonce: string; // BigInt as string
  voter: string; // Ethereum address
}

Export TypedDataDomain and TypedDataField types compatible with ethers.js v6. Create utility function createVoteMessage(proposalId: string, voteType: 'UPVOTE' | 'DOWNVOTE', nonce: bigint, voter: string): VoteMessage. Add function to generate domain separator: createVoteDomain(contractAddress: string). Include comprehensive JSDoc comments explaining EIP-712 structure.

Update packages/common/package.json to add export:
"./eip712-types": "./eip712-types.ts"

Follow the pattern established in packages/common/contract.ts for helper functions and type definitions. The file should be consistent with the monorepo structure where contracts are stored in the root contracts/ directory (currently contains UserTransactions.sol).
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test that VoteMessage structure matches EIP-712 standard. Test createVoteMessage() returns correct format with proper type conversion (voteType to boolean). Test domain separator generation with custom contract address. Validate types are compatible with ethers.js v6 Wallet.signTypedData(). Test that exported module can be imported in frontend and validator apps using 'common/eip712-types'.

tasks.title: Develop W3Governance Smart Contract
tasks.description: Create Solidity governance contract for recording proposals and verifying vote signatures on Sepolia
tasks.details:
Create contracts/W3Governance.sol using Solidity ^0.8.20, following the same patterns as UserTransactions.sol (contracts/UserTransactions.sol:1-173).

Import OpenZeppelin contracts (same as UserTransactions.sol):
- @openzeppelin/contracts/access/Ownable.sol
- @openzeppelin/contracts/security/ReentrancyGuard.sol
- @openzeppelin/contracts/security/Pausable.sol
- @openzeppelin/contracts/utils/cryptography/ECDSA.sol

Additionally import for EIP-712:
- @openzeppelin/contracts/utils/cryptography/EIP712.sol

Implement EIP712 constructor with name 'W3Uptime Governance' and version '1'.

State variables:
- mapping(uint256 => Proposal) public proposals
- uint256 public proposalCount
- bytes32 private constant VOTE_TYPEHASH = keccak256("Vote(string proposalId,bool support,uint256 nonce,address voter)")

Proposal struct: { uint256 id; address proposer; bytes32 contentHash; uint256 createdAt; uint256 votingEndsAt; uint256 upvotes; uint256 downvotes; bool finalized; bool passed; }

Functions:
- createProposal(bytes32 contentHash, uint256 votingDuration) returns (uint256) - emits ProposalCreated
- finalizeProposal(uint256 proposalId, address[] voters, bool[] votes, bytes[] signatures) - batch verify signatures using EIP-712, tally votes, determine pass/fail (requires 2/3 majority), emit ProposalFinalized
- verifyVoteSignature(string proposalId, bool support, uint256 nonce, address voter, bytes signature) returns (bool) - verify single EIP-712 signature
- getProposal(uint256 proposalId) returns (Proposal)

Events: ProposalCreated(uint256 indexed proposalId, address indexed proposer, bytes32 contentHash, uint256 votingEndsAt); ProposalFinalized(uint256 indexed proposalId, uint256 upvotes, uint256 downvotes, bool passed)

Security patterns (follow UserTransactions.sol):
- Custom errors instead of require statements (e.g., InvalidProposalId(), VotingNotEnded(), AlreadyFinalized())
- ReentrancyGuard on finalizeProposal
- onlyOwner for pause/unpause functions
- Input validation: array lengths must match, prevent finalization before voting ends, prevent duplicate finalization
- ECDSA signature recovery pattern similar to UserTransactions.sol:105-106

Deployment: Will be deployed manually using Remix IDE to Sepolia testnet (no Hardhat deployment scripts needed).
tasks.testStrategy: Manual testing in Remix IDE: (1) Deploy contract to Sepolia testnet via Remix. (2) Verify contract on Etherscan. (3) Test createProposal() emits correct event with content hash. (4) Test verifyVoteSignature() with valid EIP-712 signature returns true. (5) Test finalizeProposal() with 2/3+ majority marks passed=true. (6) Test finalizeProposal() with <2/3 majority marks passed=false. (7) Test signature verification rejects invalid signatures. (8) Test early finalization is prevented. (9) Test with multiple batch sizes (10, 50, 100 signatures). (10) Monitor gas usage for batch finalization operations via Etherscan.

tasks.title: Create Smart Contract Deployment Script
tasks.description: Deploy W3Governance contract to Sepolia testnet via Remix IDE and save deployment information
tasks.details:
Deploy W3Governance contract using Remix IDE workflow:

1. **Open Remix IDE**: Navigate to https://remix.ethereum.org in browser
2. **Import Contract Files**: 
   - Create new file `W3Governance.sol` in Remix workspace
   - Copy contract code from contracts/W3Governance.sol (to be created in Task 3)
   - Import any dependencies if needed
3. **Compile Contract**:
   - Select Solidity compiler version ^0.8.20 in Remix compiler tab
   - Enable optimization if desired (200 runs recommended)
   - Click 'Compile W3Governance.sol'
   - Verify no compilation errors
4. **Connect MetaMask to Sepolia**:
   - Install MetaMask browser extension if not installed
   - Switch MetaMask network to Sepolia Testnet
   - Ensure deployer wallet has sufficient Sepolia ETH (minimum 0.1 ETH recommended)
   - Get test ETH from Sepolia faucet if needed: https://sepoliafaucet.com
5. **Deploy Contract**:
   - Go to 'Deploy & Run Transactions' tab in Remix
   - Select 'Injected Provider - MetaMask' as environment
   - Verify connected account and network in Remix matches MetaMask
   - Enter constructor parameters if required by W3Governance contract
   - Click 'Deploy' button
   - Confirm transaction in MetaMask popup
   - Wait for deployment confirmation (2 blocks)
6. **Record Deployment Information**:
   - Copy deployed contract address from Remix console
   - Copy transaction hash from Remix console
   - Note block number and deployer address
   - Create file `deployments/sepolia-governance.json` with:
   ```json
   {
     "contractAddress": "0x...",
     "txHash": "0x...",
     "blockNumber": 123456,
     "deployer": "0x...",
     "timestamp": "2025-12-11T...",
     "network": "sepolia",
     "contractName": "W3Governance"
   }
   ```
7. **Update Environment Variables**:
   - Add `NEXT_PUBLIC_GOVERNANCE_CONTRACT_ADDRESS=0x...` to `.env` and `.env.example`
   - Update `packages/common/governance-contract.ts` to export governance contract address
8. **Verify Contract on Sepolia Etherscan**:
   - Navigate to https://sepolia.etherscan.io/address/[CONTRACT_ADDRESS]
   - Click 'Contract' tab, then 'Verify and Publish'
   - Select 'Solidity (Single File)' or 'Solidity (Standard JSON Input)'
   - Enter compiler version (0.8.20+), optimization settings
   - Paste contract source code
   - Submit for verification
   - Wait for verification confirmation

**Alternative Verification** (if Remix verification fails):
- Use Hardhat verify plugin: `npx hardhat verify --network sepolia [CONTRACT_ADDRESS] [CONSTRUCTOR_ARGS]`
- Requires ETHERSCAN_API_KEY in .env file

**Error Handling**:
- Insufficient funds: Get more Sepolia ETH from faucet
- Network issues: Check MetaMask connection, try different RPC endpoint
- Verification failures: Ensure exact compiler settings match, check constructor args
- Gas estimation failures: Increase gas limit manually in MetaMask

**Documentation**:
- Create `docs/deployment-guide.md` documenting the deployment process
- Include screenshots of successful deployment for reference
- Document any issues encountered and solutions
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual testing checklist:
1. **Pre-Deployment**: Verify contract compiles in Remix with no errors, check MetaMask connected to Sepolia, confirm sufficient test ETH balance
2. **Deployment**: Successfully deploy contract via Remix, receive transaction hash, deployment appears in MetaMask activity
3. **On-Chain Verification**: Contract address viewable on Sepolia Etherscan at https://sepolia.etherscan.io/address/[ADDRESS], transaction shows in 'Internal Txns' tab
4. **Contract Interaction**: In Remix 'Deployed Contracts' section, test calling read-only functions (e.g., getProposal(0) returns empty/default values), verify contract responds correctly
5. **File Creation**: Verify `deployments/sepolia-governance.json` exists with correct data format, environment variable added to `.env` and `.env.example`
6. **Source Verification**: Contract source code visible on Sepolia Etherscan 'Contract' tab (green checkmark), ABI visible and matches compiled output, 'Read Contract' and 'Write Contract' tabs functional on Etherscan
7. **Integration Test**: Import contract in `packages/common/governance-contract.ts`, create contract instance using deployed address, call contract functions from frontend code successfully

**Success Criteria**:
- Contract deployed to Sepolia with verified source code
- Deployment artifacts saved to `deployments/sepolia-governance.json`
- Environment variables updated with contract address
- Contract callable from Remix and Etherscan interfaces
- Documentation created for future deployments

tasks.title: Create Governance Contract Integration Module
tasks.description: Build TypeScript module in packages/common for contract ABI and helper functions
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test createGovernanceContract() returns valid Contract instance. Test GOVERNANCE_CONTRACT_ADDRESS loaded from environment variable. Test getProposalFromChain() with mock provider and mocked contract responses. Integration test on Sepolia: (1) Set NEXT_PUBLIC_GOVERNANCE_CONTRACT_ADDRESS in .env. (2) Create contract instance with AlchemyProvider. (3) Call getProposal() for existing proposal. (4) Verify response matches OnChainProposal interface. Test event listeners receive and parse ProposalCreated/ProposalFinalized events. Test error handling when environment variable not set.

tasks.title: Extend Prisma Schema for On-Chain Governance
tasks.description: Add on-chain tracking fields to Proposal model and create SignedVote model
tasks.details:
Edit packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma. Extend existing Proposal model with NULLABLE fields (backward compatible):

// Add to Proposal model
onChainId          Int?              @unique
contentHash        String?
creationTxHash     String?           @unique
creationBlockNumber Int?
finalizationTxHash String?           @unique
votingEndsAt       DateTime?
onChainStatus      OnChainStatus     @default(DRAFT)

// Add relation
signedVotes        SignedVote[]

Create new SignedVote model:
model SignedVote {
  id               String    @id @default(uuid())
  proposalId       String
  userId           String
  walletAddress    String
  voteType         VoteType  // existing enum: UPVOTE, DOWNVOTE
  signature        String    // EIP-712 signature hex string
  nonce            String    // Store as string (BigInt)
  submittedOnChain Boolean   @default(false)
  createdAt        DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt        DateTime  @updatedAt

  proposal         Proposal  @relation(fields: [proposalId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  user             User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])

  @@unique([proposalId, userId])
  @@index([proposalId])
  @@index([submittedOnChain])
  @@index([proposalId, voteType])
}

Create OnChainStatus enum:
enum OnChainStatus {
  DRAFT              // Not yet submitted on-chain
  PENDING_ONCHAIN    // Transaction submitted, awaiting confirmation
  ACTIVE             // On-chain and voting active
  PASSED             // Finalized and passed
  FAILED             // Finalized but failed
  FINALIZED          // Finalized (legacy)
}

Update User model to include signedVotes relation. Add @@index annotations for efficient queries.
tasks.testStrategy: Run 'npx prisma migrate dev --name add-onchain-governance' in packages/db. Verify migration creates new fields and tables. Test backward compatibility: existing proposals still accessible. Insert test SignedVote record. Query proposals with include: { signedVotes: true }. Verify indexes created with EXPLAIN ANALYZE on PostgreSQL.

tasks.title: Create Content Hash Utility Function
tasks.description: Build utility to generate keccak256 hash of proposal content for on-chain storage
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test: Same title+description always produces same hash. Different inputs produce different hashes. Test with special characters, emojis, multi-line descriptions. Test verifyContentHash() correctly validates matches. Test edge cases: empty strings, very long text (10,000+ chars).

tasks.title: Implement On-Chain Proposal Creation API
tasks.description: Enhance POST /api/proposals to support on-chain proposal creation with transaction handling
tasks.details:
Update apps/frontend/app/api/proposals/route.ts POST handler. Extend createProposalSchema:

const createProposalSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string().min(1),
  description: z.string().min(1),
  type: z.enum(['FEATURE_REQUEST', 'CHANGE_REQUEST']),
  tags: z.array(z.string()).optional().default([]),
  createOnChain: z.boolean().optional().default(false),
  txHash: z.string().optional(), // Transaction hash from user's wallet
});

Flow:
1. Validate request with reputation check (existing)
2. Generate contentHash using generateContentHash()
3. If createOnChain=true:
   a. Require txHash parameter
   b. Verify transaction exists and is confirmed on Sepolia using ethers.js provider
   c. Decode transaction logs to get ProposalCreated event
   d. Extract onChainId from event
   e. Calculate votingEndsAt (createdAt + 7 days)
   f. Set onChainStatus = ACTIVE
4. Create Proposal record with on-chain fields
5. Return proposal with on-chain metadata

Create helper function verifyProposalTransaction(txHash: string, contentHash: string, proposer: string): Promise<{ onChainId: number, blockNumber: number }>. Use AlchemyProvider or InfuraProvider for Sepolia. Handle errors: transaction not found, not confirmed, event not in logs, contentHash mismatch.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: (1) Create off-chain proposal (createOnChain=false) works as before. (2) Create on-chain proposal with valid txHash creates record with onChainId. (3) Invalid txHash returns 400 error. (4) ContentHash mismatch returns 400. (5) Unconfirmed transaction returns 400. Test with real Sepolia transaction.

tasks.title: Create Transaction Confirmation Listener Service
tasks.description: Build background service to listen for ProposalCreated events and update database
tasks.testStrategy: Test on local Hardhat network with mock contract events. Deploy to Sepolia testnet, create proposal via contract, verify database updates automatically. Test reconnection: kill WebSocket, verify reconnects within 30s. Test multiple rapid events don't cause race conditions.

tasks.title: Create Vote Signature Generation Endpoint
tasks.description: Build API endpoint to generate EIP-712 typed data for users to sign votes
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: (1) Call endpoint for active proposal returns valid typed data. (2) Call for inactive proposal returns 400. (3) Call after voting ended returns 400. (4) Typed data structure is valid EIP-712 format. (5) Frontend can sign returned data with MetaMask. Test nonce uniqueness across concurrent requests.

tasks.title: Implement Vote Signature Submission API
tasks.description: Create endpoint to receive, validate, and store EIP-712 vote signatures
tasks.details:
Update apps/frontend/app/api/proposals/[id]/vote/route.ts POST handler. New schema:

const voteSchema = z.object({
  voteType: z.enum(['UPVOTE', 'DOWNVOTE']),
  signature: z.string().regex(/^0x[a-fA-F0-9]{130}$/), // 65 bytes hex
  nonce: z.string(), // BigInt as string
  useSignature: z.boolean().optional().default(false)
});

Flow:
1. Validate proposal is ACTIVE and voting not ended
2. If useSignature=true:
   a. Recover signer from signature using ethers.js verifyTypedData()
   b. Verify signer matches user.walletAddress
   c. Verify message contains correct proposalId, voteType, nonce
   d. Check if user already voted (find existing SignedVote)
   e. If exists: update signature, voteType, nonce, updatedAt, set submittedOnChain=false
   f. If new: create SignedVote record
3. If useSignature=false (legacy): update/create ProposalVote as before
4. Calculate and return vote counts:
   - For signature-based: count SignedVote records grouped by voteType
   - For legacy: count ProposalVote records
5. Return { success: true, votes: { upvotes, downvotes }, userVote }

Implement signature verification:
import { verifyTypedData } from 'ethers';
const recoveredAddress = verifyTypedData(domain, types, message, signature);

Handle race conditions with database transaction. Validate nonce is recent (within 1 hour).
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: (1) Submit valid signature creates SignedVote record. (2) Submit invalid signature returns 401. (3) Change vote updates existing record. (4) Signature from wrong wallet rejected. (5) Old nonce (>1 hour) rejected. (6) Vote counts reflect signature-based votes. (7) Concurrent vote changes handled correctly. Test with MetaMask-signed data.

tasks.title: Create Batch Signature Aggregation Function
tasks.description: Build utility to collect and format all vote signatures for on-chain submission
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test with mock Prisma: (1) Returns correct parallel arrays. (2) Vote counts accurate. (3) Participation rate calculated correctly. (4) Pass threshold correct (2/3 boundary). (5) Empty votes returns empty arrays. Integration test: Create 10 test votes, verify aggregation matches.

tasks.title: Implement Proposal Finalization API Endpoint
tasks.description: Create endpoint to trigger on-chain finalization of proposals with signature batches
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test on Sepolia: (1) Create proposal, collect votes, finalize successfully. (2) Finalize before voting ends returns 400. (3) Finalize without quorum returns 400. (4) Verify transaction appears on Sepolia Etherscan. (5) Database updated correctly. (6) Signatures marked submittedOnChain=true. Test gas costs for various batch sizes (10, 50, 100 votes).

tasks.title: Create Finalization Event Listener
tasks.description: Build listener for ProposalFinalized events to update database with on-chain results
tasks.testStrategy: Test on Sepolia: Manually finalize proposal via contract directly (not through API), verify database updates via listener. Test duplicate event handling (restart listener mid-finalization). Verify idempotency: listener can process same event multiple times without corruption.

tasks.title: Build Transparency Dashboard API
tasks.description: Create API endpoint to fetch all governance data with on-chain verification links
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: (1) Returns all on-chain proposals with verification links. (2) includeOffChain=true returns DRAFT proposals. (3) Stats calculated correctly. (4) Etherscan URLs are valid format. (5) Vote counts fetched from contract match on-chain state. Test with 100+ proposals for performance (<2s response time).

tasks.title: Create Vote Verification API Endpoint
tasks.description: Build endpoint for users to verify their vote was included on-chain
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test on Sepolia: (1) User who voted on-chain sees voted=true with correct vote type. (2) User who didn't vote sees voted=false. (3) Transaction hash links to valid Etherscan page showing VoteCast event. (4) Verify getVote() response matches actual on-chain state. (5) Test with multiple voters on same proposal. (6) Test error handling when RPC fails. (7) Verify timestamp matches block timestamp.

tasks.title: Create Governance Data Export Endpoint
tasks.description: Build API endpoint to export complete governance data as JSON for independent verification
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: (1) Export all proposals returns valid JSON. (2) Filter by proposalId returns single proposal. (3) Date range filtering works. (4) File downloads with correct filename. (5) JSON structure is valid and can be parsed. (6) All vote transaction hashes are valid Etherscan links. Test file size with 100+ proposals (<20MB).

tasks.title: Create useGovernanceContract React Hook
tasks.description: Build React hook for interacting with governance smart contract from frontend
tasks.testStrategy: Component test with mock provider: (1) Hook initializes contract when MetaMask available. (2) Error set when wrong network. (3) createProposal() calls contract and returns proposalId. (4) vote() calls contract and returns txHash. (5) getProposal() fetches on-chain data. (6) getVote() returns voter's vote status. Integration test in browser: Connect MetaMask to Sepolia, call createProposal(), verify transaction succeeds, call vote(), verify vote recorded.

tasks.title: Create useVote React Hook
tasks.description: Build React hook for direct on-chain voting via MetaMask transaction
tasks.testStrategy: Component test with mocked MetaMask provider: (1) Hook connects to MetaMask when called. (2) Verifies network is Sepolia. (3) Calls contract.vote(proposalId, support). (4) Waits for transaction confirmation. (5) Returns transaction hash and block number. (6) Invalidates relevant queries on success. (7) Test error scenarios: user rejection (code 4001), wrong network, insufficient gas, proposal not found. Integration test in browser: Connect MetaMask to Sepolia, vote on active proposal, verify transaction on Etherscan, check vote recorded on-chain via contract.getVote().

tasks.title: Update Proposal Creation UI for On-Chain Submission
tasks.description: Enhance proposal creation form to support on-chain proposal creation with MetaMask
tasks.details:
Update apps/frontend/app/(user)/community/create/CreateProposalForm.tsx:

1. Add checkbox field: "Create on-chain proposal (requires gas fee)"
2. When checked:
   - Show estimated gas fee (fetch from contract.createProposal.estimateGas())
   - Display MetaMask connection status
   - Show Sepolia network requirement

3. Update form submission flow:
   - If on-chain selected:
     a. Generate contentHash from title + description
     b. Call useGovernanceContract().createProposal(contentHash)
     c. Show transaction pending state ("Waiting for confirmation...")
     d. Once confirmed, get txHash and proposalId from receipt
     e. Submit to POST /api/proposals with createOnChain=true and txHash
   - If off-chain: existing flow

4. Add UI components:
   - Badge showing "On-Chain Verified" when proposal created on-chain
   - Link to Sepolia Etherscan transaction
   - Gas fee estimator
   - Network switcher prompt if not on Sepolia

5. Form schema update:
const schema = z.object({
  title: z.string().min(10, 'Title must be at least 10 characters'),
  description: z.string().min(50, 'Description must be at least 50 characters'),
  type: z.enum(['FEATURE_REQUEST', 'CHANGE_REQUEST']),
  tags: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
  createOnChain: z.boolean().default(false)
});

6. Error handling:
   - User rejects transaction: show friendly message
   - Insufficient gas: show required amount
   - Wrong network: prompt network switch
   - Transaction fails: show error details

Use Shadcn components: Checkbox, Badge, Alert, Skeleton (for loading states). Show transaction progress with toast notifications using sonner.
tasks.testStrategy: Component test: (1) Form submits off-chain proposal without MetaMask. (2) On-chain checkbox shows gas estimate. (3) On-chain submission calls contract then API. (4) Error states render correctly. E2E test in browser: Create on-chain proposal on Sepolia, verify shows in dashboard with Etherscan link. Test user rejection flow. Test insufficient gas scenario.

tasks.title: Update Voting UI for Direct On-Chain Voting
tasks.description: Enhance proposal voting interface to support direct on-chain voting with gas payment
tasks.details:
Update apps/frontend/app/(user)/community/[id]/ProposalDetailClient.tsx to support direct on-chain voting:

1. Detect if proposal is on-chain (onChainStatus !== 'DRAFT')
2. For on-chain proposals:
   - Replace vote buttons with "Vote On-Chain" buttons
   - Show MetaMask icon on buttons
   - Display estimated gas fee before voting (fetch from contract.vote.estimateGas())
   - Show voting deadline countdown timer

3. On-chain voting flow:
   - User clicks "Upvote On-Chain" or "Downvote On-Chain"
   - Show confirmation dialog with:
     * Vote type (upvote/downvote)
     * Estimated gas fee (e.g., "~0.0008 ETH")
     * Warning: "This will cost gas and requires MetaMask"
   - User confirms → call useVote().vote({ proposalId, support })
   - Show MetaMask transaction confirmation dialog (browser-controlled)
   - Transaction states:
     * Pending: Show loading spinner with "Transaction pending..."
     * Confirmed: Show success toast with transaction hash and Etherscan link
     * Failed: Show error toast with user-friendly message
   - On success:
     * Display "Vote recorded on-chain!" success message
     * Show Etherscan transaction link
     * Fetch updated vote counts from contract
     * Show user's current vote badge
   - On error:
     * Show error toast based on error code:
       - 4001: "You rejected the transaction"
       - Insufficient gas: "Insufficient ETH for gas fees. Please add ETH to your wallet."
       - Voting ended: "Voting period has ended for this proposal"
       - Already voted: "You have already voted on this proposal. Vote changes are not allowed."
     * Log detailed error to console

4. Vote display enhancements:
   - Fetch vote counts from contract: contract.getProposal(onChainId)
   - Display "Active Voting" badge for proposals accepting votes
   - Check if user has voted: contract.getVote(proposalId, userAddress)
   - If user voted:
     * Show "You voted: [UPVOTE/DOWNVOTE]" badge
     * Display timestamp and Etherscan link to vote transaction
     * Show "Vote Locked" message (no vote changes)
   - Vote breakdown: X upvotes, Y downvotes, Z total
   - Progress bar showing upvote/downvote ratio

5. Voting period enforcement:
   - Disable voting buttons after votingEndsAt
   - Show "Voting ended [time ago]" message
   - Display final vote tally from contract

6. Network and wallet validation:
   - Check user is connected to correct network (Sepolia)
   - Show "Wrong Network" warning if on different chain
   - Display "Connect Wallet" button if not connected
   - Show user's current wallet balance

7. Add/update components:
   - VotingCountdown: Timer showing time remaining (task 24)
   - GasEstimateDisplay: Shows estimated gas cost before transaction
   - TransactionStatus: Shows pending/confirmed/failed states
   - VoteTransactionLink: Link to view vote transaction on Etherscan
   - NetworkWarning: Alert for wrong network
   - VoteConfirmDialog: Confirmation dialog before submitting vote

Use Shadcn: Button, Badge, Progress, Tooltip, Card, Separator, Alert, Dialog, AlertDialog. Use Lucide icons: CheckCircle2, XCircle, Clock, Zap (for gas), ExternalLink (for Etherscan), Wallet.

Note: This implementation uses direct on-chain voting where each user pays their own gas. Reference contracts/W3Governance.sol for contract integration.
tasks.testStrategy: Component test: (1) On-chain proposal shows on-chain voting UI with gas estimates. (2) Off-chain proposal shows legacy voting. (3) Vote transaction calls contract.vote() with correct parameters. (4) Gas estimate displayed before voting. (5) Confirmation dialog shows vote details. (6) Transaction pending state shows loading indicator. (7) Success updates UI and shows Etherscan link. (8) Error shows user-friendly message. (9) Voting disabled after deadline. (10) Wrong network shows warning. (11) Already voted shows locked state. E2E test: Connect wallet, vote on Sepolia proposal, verify transaction on Etherscan, check vote recorded on-chain via contract.getVote(), verify UI shows vote lock.

tasks.title: Create Transparency Dashboard Page
tasks.description: Build public transparency dashboard showing all governance activity with blockchain verification
tasks.details:
Create apps/frontend/app/(user)/community/dashboard/page.tsx: Server component that fetches initial data: export default async function TransparencyDashboard() { const response = await fetch(`${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL}/api/proposals/dashboard`, { cache: 'no-store' // Always fetch fresh data }); const { proposals, stats } = await response.json(); return (
); } Components to create: 1. DashboardHeader.tsx: - Display platform statistics (total proposals, on-chain %, finalized count) - Show contract address with copy button - Link to Sepolia Etherscan contract page - "Export Data" button (calls /api/proposals/export) - Note about direct on-chain voting model 2. DashboardFilters.tsx: - Filter by onChainStatus (All, Active, Passed, Failed) - Filter by type (All, Feature Request, Change Request) - Date range selector - Search by title 3. ProposalList.tsx: - Table view with columns: * ID (on-chain ID if available) * Title (truncated with tooltip) * Type * Status badges * Proposer (shortened wallet address) * Created date * Votes (upvotes/downvotes/total from contract) * Verification (link icons) 4. ProposalCard.tsx (expandable row): - Full title and description - Content hash with "Verify" button (computes hash client-side and compares) - Vote breakdown chart (recharts pie/bar chart) - Timeline: * Proposal created (tx link) * Voting started * Individual votes cast (VoteCast events with tx links) * Voting ended * Finalized (tx link) - Voter list from VoteCast events: * Wallet address * Vote type (upvote/downvote) * Transaction link * Timestamp - "View on Etherscan" buttons for all transactions 5. VerificationBadge.tsx: - Green shield icon + "Verified on-chain" for finalized proposals - Blue clock icon + "Active voting" for active - Gray icon + "Off-chain" for draft Styling: - Use Shadcn Table, Card, Badge, Button, Tabs - Color coding: Green for passed, red for failed, blue for active, gray for draft - Responsive: table on desktop, cards on mobile - Dark mode support Add export functionality: - "Export All Data" button downloads JSON from /api/proposals/export - Show toast notification on download start Public access: - No authentication required - Add meta tags for SEO - Add Open Graph tags for social sharing Note: Vote data is fetched directly from blockchain via VoteCast events, ensuring full transparency.
tasks.testStrategy: Component test: (1) Stats display correctly. (2) Filters work (client-side and API). (3) Proposal cards expand/collapse. (4) Export button downloads JSON. (5) Etherscan links are valid. (6) Vote events display correctly with transaction links. E2E test: Navigate to dashboard, filter proposals, expand card, view voter list with tx links, click Etherscan link (opens in new tab), export data. Test with 100+ proposals for performance. Verify public access (no auth required).

(5) Tooltip appears on hover with correct text. (6) Etherscan link rendered when txHash provided. (7) Different sizes render correctly. Visual regression test for all variants.

(5) Handles invalid dates gracefully. Test with future date (7 days), near future (1 hour), past date. Visual test for formatting consistency.

(5) Edge cases: 0 voters, exactly at threshold, over 100%. Visual test: verify yellow line position, green color when met.

(3) Success shows green checkmark and vote details with Etherscan link. (4) No vote shows gray X. (5) Error displays user-friendly message. Integration test: Verify vote for on-chain proposal after voting, check Etherscan link opens correctly and shows VoteCast event.

tasks.title: Create Automated Finalization Scheduler
tasks.description: Build cron job script to automatically finalize proposals when voting period ends
tasks.testStrategy: Test locally: (1) Create proposal with votingEndsAt in past. (2) Vote on-chain with multiple wallets. (3) Run script manually. (4) Verify proposal finalized on-chain. (5) Check database updated. (6) Test quorum failure scenario (< 50% votes). (7) Test with multiple proposals. (8) Test error handling (insufficient gas, network issues). Deploy to staging, verify cron runs every 15 minutes. Monitor logs for 24 hours.

tasks.title: Setup Platform Wallet for Finalization
tasks.description: Configure secure platform wallet to pay gas for proposal finalization transactions
tasks.details:
1. Generate new Ethereum wallet for platform finalization:
   - Use ethers.js: `const wallet = Wallet.createRandom()`
   - Save mnemonic securely (encrypted file, hardware security module, AWS KMS)
   - Export private key for .env configuration

2. Fund wallet with Sepolia ETH:
   - Obtain Sepolia ETH from faucet: https://sepoliafaucet.com/
   - Transfer at least 0.5 ETH to cover gas costs
   - Set up monitoring for low balance alerts (<0.1 ETH)

3. Environment variable configuration:
   Add to .env (NEVER commit to git):
   ```
   PLATFORM_SIGNER_PRIVATE_KEY=0x...
   PLATFORM_SIGNER_ADDRESS=0x...
   ```

4. Create wallet monitoring script scripts/check-wallet-balance.ts:
   ```typescript
   import { AlchemyProvider } from 'ethers';
   
   async function checkBalance() {
     const provider = new AlchemyProvider('sepolia', process.env.ALCHEMY_API_KEY);
     const balance = await provider.getBalance(process.env.PLATFORM_SIGNER_ADDRESS!);
     const ethBalance = Number(balance) / 1e18;
     
     console.log(`Platform wallet balance: ${ethBalance} ETH`);
     
     if (ethBalance < 0.1) {
       console.warn('⚠️  LOW BALANCE ALERT: Please fund wallet!');
       // Send email/Slack alert
     }
   }
   
   checkBalance();
   ```

5. Security measures:
   - Add .env to .gitignore (verify it's there)
   - Restrict server access to private key file
   - Use environment-specific wallets (staging vs production)
   - Set up key rotation policy (every 90 days)
   - Enable 2FA on any cloud services storing keys
   - Consider using AWS KMS or HashiCorp Vault for production

6. Documentation:
   - Document wallet address in docs/deployment.md
   - Create runbook for wallet funding process
   - Document emergency key rotation procedure

7. Monitoring:
   - Add balance check to daily cron job
   - Log all transactions from platform wallet
   - Set up alerts for unusual spending patterns

Note: Platform wallet only pays for finalization transactions. Users pay their own gas for voting transactions.
tasks.testStrategy: Test wallet: (1) Verify private key loads correctly from .env. (2) Check balance retrieval works. (3) Send test transaction on Sepolia. (4) Verify transaction appears on Etherscan. (5) Test balance monitoring script alerts at <0.1 ETH. Security audit: Confirm .env not in git, private key encrypted at rest, access logs enabled.

tasks.title: Add Gas Estimation and Error Handling
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive gas estimation and error handling for all on-chain operations
tasks.testStrategy: Test gas estimation: (1) Estimate creation gas for proposal. (2) Estimate vote gas for on-chain vote. (3) Estimate finalization gas. (4) Verify estimates within 20% of actual gas used. Error handling tests: (1) Insufficient funds shows correct message. (2) User rejection handled gracefully. (3) Network error triggers retry. (4) Contract revert reasons parsed correctly. Integration test: Create proposal with low wallet balance, verify user warned. Vote with insufficient gas, verify error message actionable.

tasks.title: Create Admin Monitoring Dashboard for Finalization
tasks.description: Build admin page to monitor finalization status and handle failures
tasks.details:
Create apps/frontend/app/(user)/admin/governance/page.tsx (protected route, admin only):

Server component fetching admin data:
- List of proposals pending finalization
- Recent finalization attempts (success/failure)
- Platform wallet balance
- Gas cost statistics
- Error logs

Components:

1. PendingFinalizationsTable:
   - Proposal ID, title, voting ended time, on-chain vote count, quorum status
   - Actions: "Finalize Now" (manual trigger), "Mark as Failed"
   - Highlight proposals overdue for finalization (>24h past voting end)

2. FinalizationHistoryTable:
   - Recent finalization attempts
   - Columns: Proposal, timestamp, tx hash, gas used, status, error (if failed)
   - Filter by success/failure
   - Export to CSV

3. WalletMonitor:
   - Current balance with color coding (green >0.3, yellow 0.1-0.3, red <0.1)
   - Transaction history from platform wallet
   - "Fund Wallet" instructions with QR code
   - Balance trend chart (last 30 days)

4. ErrorAlerts:
   - List of recent errors requiring attention
   - Categorized by type (gas issues, contract errors, network errors)
   - "Retry" button for failed finalizations
   - "Dismiss" and "Investigate" actions

5. OnChainVoteStats:
   - Show total on-chain votes for pending proposals
   - Compare to expected participation
   - Highlight low participation proposals

API endpoints:

POST /api/admin/governance/finalize-now
- Manually trigger finalization for specific proposal
- Admin authentication required
- Validate admin role

GET /api/admin/governance/stats
- Return finalization statistics
- Success rate, avg gas cost, pending count

GET /api/admin/governance/errors
- Fetch recent finalization errors
- Include stack traces and context

Security:
- Protect routes with admin role check
- Add audit logging for manual actions
- Rate limit manual finalization (1 per minute per admin)

Notifications:
- Email admin on finalization failure
- Slack webhook for critical errors
- Daily summary report

Note: Dashboard focuses on finalization monitoring since votes are directly on-chain and don't require platform intervention.

(5) Error list shows recent failures. (6) Export functionality works. (7) On-chain vote counts fetched correctly. Test notifications: Trigger finalization failure, verify email sent. Test rate limiting on manual actions.

tasks.title: Update Documentation with On-Chain Governance Guide
tasks.description: Create comprehensive documentation for direct on-chain governance feature
tasks.details:
Create docs/governance-transparency.md:

# On-Chain Governance Transparency

## Overview
W3Uptime uses blockchain technology to ensure transparent, verifiable governance. All proposals and votes are recorded directly on the Sepolia Ethereum testnet using a direct on-chain voting model.

## Voting Model: Direct On-Chain

Unlike gasless signature-based systems, W3Uptime uses **direct on-chain voting** where:
- Each vote is a blockchain transaction
- Voters pay their own gas fees (~$0.50-1.00)
- Votes are immediately visible on-chain
- No batch submission or signature aggregation needed
- Full transparency and real-time verification

## For Users

### Creating an On-Chain Proposal
1. Navigate to Community > Submit Proposal
2. Fill in title, description, type, and tags
3. Check "Create on-chain proposal"
4. Click Submit - MetaMask will prompt for transaction signature
5. Pay gas fee (estimated: ~$0.50 on Sepolia)
6. Wait for confirmation (~15 seconds)
7. Proposal is now verifiable on Etherscan

### Voting on Proposals
1. Open any active on-chain proposal
2. Click "Upvote On-Chain" or "Downvote On-Chain"
3. Review gas estimate in confirmation dialog
4. Confirm in MetaMask and pay gas fee (~$0.50-1.00)
5. Your vote is recorded immediately on blockchain
6. View your vote transaction on Etherscan
7. **Note**: Vote changes are NOT allowed after submission

### Verifying Your Vote
1. After voting, your vote is immediately visible on-chain
2. Click "Verify My Vote" on proposal page
3. System fetches your vote directly from blockchain
4. View your VoteCast transaction on Etherscan
5. Verify vote timestamp and block number

### Understanding Governance Rules
- **Voting Period**: 7 days from proposal creation
- **Quorum Requirement**: 50% of users must participate
- **Passing Threshold**: 2/3 (66.67%) of votes must be upvotes
- **Finalization**: Automatic within 24 hours after voting ends
- **Vote Changes**: NOT allowed (each address can vote once)
- **Gas Costs**: Users pay gas for votes, platform pays for finalization

## For Developers

### Smart Contract Details
- **Network**: Sepolia Testnet (Chain ID: 11155111)
- **Contract Address**: [See .env.example]
- **Verified Source**: https://sepolia.etherscan.io/address/...
- **Voting Model**: Direct on-chain (no EIP-712 signatures)

### Architecture
```
User → MetaMask → Smart Contract (vote tx) → Blockchain
                     ↓
              VoteCast Event
                     ↓
           Event Listener → Database (cache)
```

### Key Technologies
- **Direct On-Chain Voting**: Each vote is a blockchain transaction
- **ethers.js v6**: Blockchain interaction library
- **Sepolia**: Ethereum testnet
- **Prisma ORM**: Database management (vote caching)

### API Endpoints
- `POST /api/proposals` - Create proposal (on-chain or off-chain)
- `GET /api/proposals/[id]/verify-vote` - Verify vote on-chain via contract.getVote()
- `GET /api/proposals/dashboard` - Transparency dashboard data
- `GET /api/proposals/export` - Export governance data with VoteCast events

### Database Schema
See IMPLEMENTATION_CHANGES.md for Prisma schema updates.
- SignedVote model removed (no signatures)
- VoteCache model added for on-chain vote caching

### Smart Contract Functions
```solidity
function createProposal(bytes32 contentHash, uint256 votingDuration) external returns (uint256)
function vote(uint256 proposalId, bool support) external
function getVote(uint256 proposalId, address voter) external view returns (bool hasVoted, bool support)
function finalizeProposal(uint256 proposalId) external
function getProposal(uint256 proposalId) external view returns (Proposal)
```

### Running Finalization Scheduler
```bash
# Manual run
npm run finalize-proposals

# Setup cron (Linux/Mac)
crontab -e
*/15 * * * * cd /path/to/w3uptime && npm run finalize-proposals >> logs/finalization.log 2>&1
```

### Testing
```bash
# Smart contract tests
cd contracts && npx hardhat test

# Integration tests
npm run test:integration
```

## For Validators

### Why Direct On-Chain Voting?
- **Maximum Transparency**: All votes immediately visible on blockchain
- **Real-Time Verification**: Anyone can verify votes at any time
- **Simplicity**: No signature aggregation or batch submission complexity
- **Immutability**: Votes cannot be altered once submitted
- **Trust**: No reliance on platform to submit signatures

### Trade-offs
- **Gas Costs**: Users pay gas (~$0.50-1.00 per vote on Sepolia)
- **Finality**: Votes cannot be changed after submission
- **Scalability**: Higher cost than signature-based systems

### Viewing Transparency Dashboard
Visit /community/dashboard to see:
- All on-chain proposals
- Real-time vote counts from blockchain
- Individual voter addresses and transactions
- Etherscan verification links for all votes
- Platform statistics

### Exporting Governance Data
Click "Export Data" on dashboard to download complete JSON export including:
- All proposals and metadata
- VoteCast event data with transaction hashes
- Voter addresses and vote types
- Block numbers and timestamps

## Troubleshooting

### "Please switch to Sepolia network"
1. Open MetaMask
2. Click network dropdown
3. Select "Sepolia" (or add if not listed)

### "Insufficient funds for gas"
- Get Sepolia ETH from faucet: https://sepoliafaucet.com/
- Needed for both creating proposals AND voting

### "Transaction would fail"
- Check voting period hasn't ended
- Verify proposal is in ACTIVE status
- Ensure you haven't already voted
- Try refreshing page

### "Already voted"
- Direct on-chain voting does NOT support vote changes
- Each address can only vote once per proposal
- This ensures vote integrity and prevents manipulation

### Vote not showing as verified
- Votes are verified immediately via contract.getVote()
- Check you're connected with the wallet you voted from
- Verify transaction confirmed on Etherscan
- Contact support if issue persists

## Security Considerations

### For Users
- Never share your private key
- Verify contract address before signing
- Only sign transactions you understand
- Use hardware wallet for large holdings
- Be aware votes are permanent (cannot change)

### For Platform
- Platform wallet private key stored in encrypted .env
- Regular security audits of smart contracts
- Rate limiting on API endpoints
- Monitoring for unusual activity
- Only platform pays finalization gas, users pay vote gas

## Comparison: Direct vs Gasless Voting

| Feature | Direct On-Chain | Gasless (EIP-712) |
|---------|----------------|-------------------|
| User Gas Cost | ~$0.50-1.00 per vote | $0 (free) |
| Vote Visibility | Immediate | After batch submission |
| Vote Changes | Not allowed | Allowed until finalization |
| Complexity | Simple | Signature aggregation |
| Trust Model | Trustless | Trust platform for batch |
| Verification | Real-time | After finalization |

W3Uptime uses **Direct On-Chain** for maximum transparency.

## Future Enhancements
- Mainnet deployment
- Delegation support
- Proposal deposit requirements
- Timelocked execution
- Multi-signature admin controls
- L2 deployment for lower gas costs

Update README.md to reference this document. Add link to transparency dashboard in main navigation.
